basil.name: Basil
basil.description: Basil is a popular aromatic herb commonly used in culinary dishes, especially in Italian and Mediterranean cuisine. It features bright green, tender leaves and produces small white flowers. Basil is easy to grow and thrives in warm, sunny environments.
basil.wateringSchedule: Water regularly to keep soil consistently moist but not waterlogged; typically 2-3 times per week depending on weather.
basil.soilType: Well-draining, fertile soil rich in organic matter
basil.plantingGuidelines: Plant basil outdoors after the last frost when soil temperatures are above 50°F. Sow seeds 1/4 inch deep or transplant seedlings, spacing plants 12-18 inches apart.
growingTips: Pinch off flower buds to encourage leaf growth
Harvest leaves regularly to promote bushiness
Avoid overhead watering to reduce fungal disease
Mulch to retain soil moisture and suppress weeds

sugar snap peas.name: Sugar Snap Pea
sugar snap peas.description: Sugar snap peas are a crisp, sweet vegetable prized for their edible pods and seeds. They are easy to grow, thriving in cool weather and producing abundant harvests in spring and early summer. Their crunchy pods are enjoyed fresh, steamed, or stir-fried.
sugar snap peas.wateringSchedule: Water regularly to keep soil evenly moist, about 1 inch per week; avoid waterlogging.
sugar snap peas.soilType: Well-drained, fertile loam with a neutral to slightly alkaline pH (6.0-7.5)
sugar snap peas.plantingGuidelines: Plant seeds directly outdoors 4-6 weeks before the last spring frost, spacing seeds 1 inch apart and rows 18-24 inches apart. Provide support with trellises or netting as vines grow.
growingTips: Harvest pods when they are plump but before seeds fully mature
Mulch to retain moisture and suppress weeds
Rotate crops yearly to prevent disease
Avoid planting in hot weather to prevent poor pod set

sugar snap pea.name: Sugar Snap Pea
sugar snap pea.description: Sugar snap peas are a type of edible-pod pea known for their sweet, crisp pods and tender peas inside. They are a cool-season vegetable, popular for fresh eating, stir-fries, and salads. The plants are vining and benefit from support as they grow.
sugar snap pea.wateringSchedule: Water regularly to keep soil evenly moist, about 1 inch per week; do not let soil dry out completely.
sugar snap pea.soilType: Well-drained, loamy soil rich in organic matter; prefers slightly acidic to neutral pH (6.0-7.0).
sugar snap pea.plantingGuidelines: Sow seeds directly outdoors 2-4 weeks before the last expected spring frost, 1 inch deep and 2 inches apart. Provide a trellis or support for vines to climb.
growingTips: Mulch around plants to retain moisture and suppress weeds.
Harvest pods when they are plump and crisp, before seeds fully mature.
Avoid handling plants when wet to prevent disease spread.
Rotate crops yearly to reduce soil-borne diseases.

tomato.name: Tomato
tomato.description: Tomatoes are warm-season fruiting plants prized for their juicy, flavorful fruits used in a wide range of culinary dishes. They grow as annuals in most climates and come in many varieties, including cherry, plum, and beefsteak. Tomatoes are highly productive when given proper care and conditions.
tomato.wateringSchedule: Water deeply 1-2 times per week, keeping soil consistently moist but not waterlogged
tomato.soilType: Well-draining, fertile loam rich in organic matter
tomato.plantingGuidelines: Start seeds indoors 6-8 weeks before the last frost date or transplant seedlings after all danger of frost has passed. Plant 18-24 inches apart, burying the stem deeply to encourage strong root growth.
growingTips: Mulch to retain moisture and suppress weeds
Stake or cage plants for support
Prune lower leaves to improve air circulation
Rotate crops yearly to prevent disease
Feed with balanced fertilizer during growth
